ignorePatterns:
node_modules/
dist/
build/
coverage/
*.min.js
*.map
*.tsbuildinfo
pnpm-lock.yaml
package-lock.json
plugin-runtime/*.cjs
plugin-runtime/node_modules/
.beads/

rules.rule: ONE plugin, two runtime modes dispatched at startup by `TEAMKB_API_URL` (resolved in `src/mode.ts`). Unset / empty / whitespace-only / an unexpanded `${...}` shell placeholder -> LOCAL (in-process ~/.teamkb, full brain_* surface); any other non-empty value -> TEAM (remote proxy to the one shared brain). The `${...}` guard is load-bearing: without it a literal "${TEAMKB_API_URL}" is treated as a real URL and a misconfigured host silently runs the WRONG brain. Flag any change that removes the empty / placeholder guards, makes the dispatch ambiguous, or eagerly loads both modes (only the selected mode must load).

Team mode is DEPENDENCY-FREE: import ONLY `node:crypto`, `fetch`, `zod`, and the MCP SDK — NEVER `@qmd-team-intent-kb/*` or anything that pulls `better-sqlite3`. The team bundle must run from a marketplace clone with zero build. And team-mode errors must SURFACE to the caller (`ok:false` + a message) — never a silent `count:0` / empty result that masks an auth or network failure as 'no hits'. Flag a heavy/native import added to remote-server, and any error path swallowed into an empty success.

Local-mode writers (`brain_capture` / `brain_govern` / `brain_transition`) take the `~/.teamkb/.write.lock` via flock(2), which MUST interoperate with the nightly compile cron's `/usr/bin/flock` on the same lock file (advisory lock, same path). Writers fail CLOSED on any lock error — never proceed unlocked. Flag any change that drops the lock, moves the lock path so it no longer interoperates with the cron, or lets a writer continue when the lock cannot be acquired.

`plugin-runtime/governed-brain.cjs` is a GENERATED single-file bundle produced by `build.mjs` from `src/`. Never hand-edit it, and never suggest editing it directly to change behavior. Any `src/**` change must be followed by a rebuild + commit of the bundle, and the `gsb.lock.json` version tuple stays consistent (smoke/check-lock.mjs enforces plugin/package/marketplace/.mcp version agreement). Flag a src change with no corresponding bundle rebuild.

In local mode the plugin runs the govern -> retrieve loop in-process; the `candidates` table is insert-only Tier-A source of truth (005-AT-ARCH), and the govern pipeline stays deterministic (no LLM call below the spool). Never make govern.ts destructively delete candidates or retire flagged/rejected captures with a hard delete — retirement is marker-based; keep flagged/rejected rows for admin review. The model proposes (capture); deterministic govern disposes.

The audit chain is append-only + SHA-256 hash-chained. NEVER suggest re-hashing the chain, editing past events, or 'fixing' the 155 carried CHAIN_FORK breaks by re-hashing (ratified D5 — benign same-timestamp ordering, all hashes intact). `brain_audit_verify` reports an honest 3-state banner: ok:true with N benign forks is NOT tamper; ok:false IS real tamper — investigate, never migrate. Do not resurrect the old false 'TAMPER DETECTED' klaxon.

No plaintext bearer token, API key, or secret in any committed file — including 'for testing'. Team mode reads its per-user token from env / config at runtime, never hardcoded; never log a token. The remote brain's tokens are scrypt-hashed at rest server-side — the plugin never stores or echoes a plaintext secret.

The required CI checks are the merge gate: `smoke.yml` (full-chain zero-egress smoke + `check-lock.mjs` version-drift guard) plus the vitest + eslint + typecheck job. Never suggest weakening a smoke assertion, disabling eslint/typecheck, deleting a test, or loosening the lock guard — unless the same PR ships a strictly stronger equivalent.

NEVER introduce or accept the words tamper-proof / immutable / non-repudiation (for local mode) / blockchain as product claims — in code, comments, tool descriptions, or docs (see this repo's CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md audit-honesty rule). The chain is tamper-EVIDENT only: integrity + ordering + rewrite-detection. Keep 'tamper-evident'. Flag any diff that softens the honesty framing or overclaims what a receipt proves.

PRs follow 013-OD-STND: What / Why + decision rationale / Layer(s) touched (compile / spool / govern / receipts / plugin surface) + any invariant changed / Verification & evidence (linked artifact) / Risk / Operational impact / Follow-up / Refs. Flag a non-trivial PR missing the Layer(s)-touched declaration or the evidence link, and any bare `fix`/`update` subject.
